chunk_summary: Guidance on integrating catch-up immunization into follow-up care for children with uncomplicated moderate wasting, emphasizing workflow, documentation, and proactive linkage to immunization drives.
learning_objectives: Understand the workflow for catch-up immunization during MAM follow-up visits.
Learn how to document and verify immunization status in children with moderate wasting.
Recognize the importance of linking underimmunized children to special immunization drives like Mission Indradhanush.
Identify contraindications and considerations for vaccination in children with moderate wasting.
key_facts: Immunization status should be documented at day 0 and week 4 follow-up.
Vaccination is recommended for clinically stable children with uncomplicated moderate wasting.
Missed vaccine doses are given as catch-up without restarting the series.
Mild intercurrent illness is not a contraindication for vaccination.
Proactive linkage to Mission Indradhanush and VHSND is advised for migrant families or those missing sessions.
reference_quote: Missed doses are given as catch-up, following schedule guidance at the immunization site. (Source: National Immunization Schedule, India)
document_key_topics: Catch-up immunization workflow for MAM follow-up
Documentation and verification of immunization status
Vaccination guidelines for children with moderate wasting
